entries.title: On John 1:1515
entries.excerpt: Or he introduces this, as if to say, Do not suppose that we bear witness to this out of gratitude, because we were with Him a long time, and partook of His table; for John who had never seen Him before, nor tarried with Him, bare witness to Him. The Evangelist repeats John’s testimony many times here and there, because he was held in such admiration by the Jews. Other Evangelists refer to the old prophets, and say, This was done that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet. But he introduces a loftier, and later witness, not intending to make the servant vouch for the master,…

entries.title: On John 1:1515
entries.excerpt: If the words, made before me, referred to His coming into being, it was superfluous to add, For He was before me. For who would be so foolish as not to know, that if He was made before him, He was before him. It would have been more correct to say, He was before me, because He was made before me. The expression then, He was made before me, must be taken in the sense of honour: only that which was to take place, he speaks of as having taken place already, after the style of the old Prophets, who commonly talk of the future as the past.

entries.title: On John 1:1515
entries.excerpt: Or this does not refer to the birth from Mary; for Christ was born, when this was said by John; but to His coming for the work of preaching. He then saith, is madea before me; that is, is more illustrious, more honourable; as if he said, Do not suppose me greater than He, because I came first to preach.

entries.title: On John 1:1515
entries.excerpt: It does not mean—He was made before I was made; but He is preferred to me.

entries.title: On John 1:1515
entries.excerpt: He saith, Who cometh after me, that is, as to the time of His birth. John was six months before Christ, according to His humanity.

entries.title: On John 1:1515
entries.excerpt: The Arians infer from this word, that the Son of God is not begotten of the Father, but made like any other creature.

entries.title: On John 1:1515
entries.excerpt: He had said before that there was a man sent to bear witness; now he gives definitely the forerunner’s own testimony, which plainly declared the excellence of His Human Nature and the Eternity of His Godhead. John bare witness of Him.
